meaning: adj.未加装饰的 简朴的
examples: Early in the Uruk period, the use of undecorated utilitarian pottery was probably the result of specialized mass production.
He was glad that he liked the country undecorated, hard, and stripped of its finery.
They are interesting for the fact that they are very plain and undecorated for the time, with only one plain central panel at the back and no armrests.
The undecorated fir tree in the dining room told me that Christmas had not yet arrived.
When your name was called, you were passed through a metal detector and ferried upstairs to an undecorated 6-by-6 cubicle.
